name: Osso
what_it_does: Open-source enterprise SSO service that adds SAML-based single sign-on to any application, handling the complexity of per-customer IdP configuration and OAuth 2.0 token issuance.
use_cases: Add enterprise SAML SSO to a SaaS product so customers can log in via their corporate identity provider (Okta, Azure AD, Google Workspace)
Manage per-customer IdP configurations programmatically via API without building a custom SAML library
Issue OAuth 2.0 tokens after SAML authentication so the rest of the application can use standard OAuth flows
Provide an admin onboarding UI for enterprise customers to self-configure their SAML connection
Migrate enterprise authentication to SAML without rewriting the application's auth layer
not_for: Consumer-facing authentication (social login, email/password) — Osso is purpose-built for enterprise SAML SSO only
Teams that need SCIM directory sync, JIT provisioning, or OIDC federation — Osso's scope is narrower than full-featured identity platforms like WorkOS or Auth0
Organizations that cannot self-host or manage their own infrastructure — Osso requires running your own service
best_when: You are a developer at a B2B SaaS company that needs to ship enterprise SAML SSO quickly without building SAML parsing, certificate management, and IdP config UI from scratch.
avoid_when: You need a fully managed enterprise identity platform with directory sync, audit logs, and OIDC support — WorkOS or Auth0 Enterprise will serve you better.

auth.notes: OAuth 2.0 client credentials for machine-to-machine API calls. API key for admin operations. Standard Bearer token pattern.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Fully open-source; self-hosted is free with no usage limits
pricing.paid_tiers: No official cloud-hosted paid tier; self-hosted only
pricing.notes: Project appears to have reduced activity as of 2023-2024; evaluate maintenance status before adopting for production use.

performance.rate_limits: No published rate limits; depends on self-hosted infrastructure

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Documentation is relatively sparse compared to commercial alternatives; error messages from the API are standard Rails JSON errors but not always developer-friendly for SAML-specific failures.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: IdP configuration create/update calls are idempotent by customer identifier. Authentication flows are stateful and session-based, not idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: SAML certificate rotation at the IdP requires manual update in Osso configuration — no automated certificate refresh mechanism exists
The OAuth token issued after SAML login is short-lived; agents must handle token refresh or re-initiate the SAML flow
Project maintenance cadence has slowed; check GitHub for open issues before deploying to production
SDK coverage is limited to Ruby and Node; other languages require direct REST API integration which is less documented
Multi-tenant SAML configuration requires careful customer isolation — misconfiguration can allow cross-tenant login in some edge cases

security_score_components.security_notes: Self-hosted open-source Ruby/Rails application; security depends entirely on operator's deployment practices. SAML assertion handling is security-critical code — validate library versions and CVEs before deploying. No formal SOC2 or compliance certifications.
